characters.biography_short: Caudillo hispanogodo que lideró la resistencia cristiana en Covadonga (c.722). Figura fundacional del núcleo político que dará origen al reino de Asturias; las fuentes lo presentan como guerrero, fugitivo tras Guadalete y posteriormente jefe de los rebeldes en la cornisa cantábrica.

voice_profile.tone: raspy, direct, authoritative; rural caudillo voice with spare rhetoric

visual_profile.hair: dark, likely unkempt (inferred from mountain-warrior image)
visual_profile.clothing: practical mountain cloak, leather and wool, simple harness and cross-emblazoned standard (inferred)
visual_profile.style_notes: Rugged, functional garb appropriate to a mountain war-leader; visual shorthand should emphasize endurance and austerity.

state.location: Covadonga / Cangas de Onís (primary historical locus)

emotional_state.trigger_summary: Defence of his people and survival after defeat at Guadalete; establishment of a safe enclave in the mountains (Covadonga)

arc_state.external_goal: secure a safe political/military base in Asturias and resist Muslim control
arc_state.internal_need: to transform personal survival/loyalty into recognized leadership for a broader community
arc_state.contradiction: celebrated as heroic in chronicles but historically may have been a local caudillo with limited centralized power
arc_state.change_vector: from fugitive warrior to foundational regional leader

goals.label: defend_refugees_and_local_population
goals.summary: Led a group of Christian fugitives/rebels who took refuge in Covadonga and resisted a larger Muslim force.

source_refs.excerpt: LA BATALLA DE COVADONGA Estamos en 722. ... los rebeldes cristianos, efectivamente pocos y mal armados, pero fuertes en un...
Así, en fin, se planteó la batalla. Los cristianos, pocos y sin alimentos; los moros, muchos y bien armados.

goals.label: became_symbolic_king
goals.summary: Covadonga elevated Pelayo's status: the victory is presented as making him a de facto king and foundational leader of Asturias.

source_refs.section: evt-083001 / Covadonga hizo verdaderamente rey a Pelayo
source_refs.excerpt: Covadonga hizo verdaderamente rey a Pelayo. La tradición atribuye al caudillo un hecho sobrenatural ...
Los cristianos ganaron. El triunfo de Pelayo hizo que otros muchos... se sumaran a la rebelión.

goals.label: fugitive_after_Guadalete
goals.summary: Pelayo appears in sources as a fugitive after the defeat at Guadalete and as a former follower of Rodrigo.

source_refs.section: evt-081401 / Así pues, ahí está ese Pelayo, fugitivo tras la derrota de Guadalete
source_refs.excerpt: Así pues, ahí está ese Pelayo, fugitivo tras la derrota de Guadalete. Pelayo era un guerrero del bando de Don Rodrigo.

source_refs.section: evt-081901 / Fiel a sus compromisos, Pelayo comparece junto a Rodrigo en Guadalete
source_refs.excerpt: Fiel a sus compromisos, Pelayo comparece junto a Rodrigo en Guadalete. Conocerá el sabor amargo de la derrota.

goals.label: succession_and_death
goals.summary: Pelayo died circa 737 and was succeeded by his son Favila.

source_refs.section: evt-073701 / Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila
source_refs.excerpt: Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila.

fears.label: fear_loss_of_community
fears.summary: Sources imply a fear of losing the small communities sheltered in the mountains to a more organized occupying power (taxation and hostages cited as triggers for revolt).

source_refs.section: evt-072201 / Los indicios ... diplomas ... pioneros en valles expuestos al peligro moro
source_refs.excerpt: Los indicios son, a saber: diplomas de remotas fundaciones monacales que nos hablan de pioneros en valles expuestos al peligro moro...

source_refs.section: chk-0152 / Por qué se levantaron aquellos astures
source_refs.excerpt: Por qué se levantaron aquellos astures ... la exigencia mora de que los asturianos tributaran con impuestos y con rehenes.

contradictions.label: legend_vs_fragmentary_sources
contradictions.summary: The traditional, often miraculous, narrative of Pelayo (e.g., supernatural protection at Covadonga) contrasts with the fragmentary and partial nature of the chronicles.

source_refs.excerpt: Fantasía, mala ignorancia, manipulación política? De todo un poco, tal vez. La verdad es que de aquella Asturias y de aquel Pelayo sabemos lo que nos dicen las crónicas, que no es mucho.

behavior_patterns.label: mountain_defense_tactics
behavior_patterns.summary: Uses the mountainous terrain to offset numerical inferiority: defensive ambush, holding narrow passes and caves.

source_refs.excerpt: Pero el terreno jugaba a favor de los cristianos: mover a un... (riesgo para el atacante).

relationships.summary: Father of Favila; Favila succeeded Pelayo as ruler of Asturias after Pelayo's death.

source_refs.section: evt-073701 / Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila
source_refs.excerpt: Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila.

relationships.summary: Father of Ermesinda (identified in sources as a daughter of Pelayo).

source_refs.excerpt: Lo único que nos consta es que una hija de Pelayo, Ermesinda...

relationships.summary: Opponent on the Muslim side in the pitched encounter described at Covadonga/related engagements.

source_refs.excerpt: Así, en fin, se planteó la batalla... actors: don_oppas, al_qama, pelayo

internal_needs.label: need_legitimacy
internal_needs.summary: Pelayo likely needed to convert local wartime leadership and loyalty into a more stable political legitimacy (foundation of a ruling lineage).

source_refs.section: evt-105101 / la corona pertenecía por derecho de preferencia a la estirpe de Pelayo
source_refs.excerpt: Más bien hay que pensar que la corona pertenecía por derecho de preferencia a la estirpe de Pelayo ...
El triunfo de Pelayo hizo que otros muchos... se sumaran a la rebelión.

private_state.label: stoic_resolve
private_state.summary: A private orientation toward stoic endurance and minimal rhetoric; sources emphasize rugged survival more than courtly culture.

source_refs.excerpt: Pelayo, dónde estás?". El interpelado se asomó a una ventana y respondió: "Aquí estoy".

likely_arc.label: fugitive_to_founder
likely_arc.summary: Character arc: from fugitive warrior (post-Guadalete) to foundational regional leader/king (Covadonga) whose victory enables later dynasty-building.

source_refs.excerpt: así pues, ahí está ese Pelayo, fugitivo tras la derrota de Guadalete.

source_refs.section: evt-083001 / Covadonga hizo verdaderamente rey a Pelayo
source_refs.excerpt: Covadonga hizo verdaderamente rey a Pelayo.

event_records.change_summary: From small-group fugitives to a recognized military victory that elevates Pelayo's leadership and attracts followers.

observed_from_source.label: covadonga_victory_observed
observed_from_source.summary: Sources record a Christian victory at Covadonga (c.722) in which Pelayo leads a small force against a larger Muslim contingent.

source_refs.excerpt: LA BATALLA DE COVADONGA Estamos en 722 ... los rebeldes cristianos, efectivamente pocos y mal armados, pero fuertes en un...
Así, en fin, se planteó la batalla.

inferred_from_source.label: covadonga_symbolic_meaning_inferred
inferred_from_source.summary: The narrative framing of Covadonga in the sources functions to symbolize divine favor and the legitimacy of Pelayo's leadership; this is more interpretive than strictly factual.

source_refs.section: evt-083001 / Covadonga hizo verdaderamente rey a Pelayo
source_refs.excerpt: La tradición atribuye al caudillo un hecho sobrenatural que tuvo lugar en Covadonga y que expresó el favor divino para con los rebeldes.
Fantasía, mala ignorancia, manipulación política? De todo un poco, tal vez.
LA BATALLA DE COVADONGA Estamos en 722 ...

characters.biography_short: Hijo de Pelayo, successor as ruler of Asturias after Pelayo's death (sources give few details; chroniclers record his succession and brief reign and later widow and daughter).

voice_profile.tone: younger, less seasoned; deferential and unsettled

visual_profile.clothing: early royal vestments mixed with warrior trappings (inferred)
visual_profile.style_notes: Portray as brief, transitional ruler; not heavily armored in pageantry—more a local aristocrat elevated by lineage.

state.location: Cangas de Onís (burial in local church recorded)

emotional_state.trigger_summary: Brief and poorly documented reign following the founder; sources imply limited political footprint.

arc_state.external_goal: maintain the legacy left by Pelayo
arc_state.internal_need: to transition from being a successor in name to a legitimate ruler (largely unrealized in sources)
arc_state.contradiction: recorded as successor but 'anodine' in later chronicling
arc_state.change_vector: succession followed by rapid disappearance from the central narrative

goals.label: succession
goals.summary: Favila succeeds Pelayo after Pelayo's death in c.737.

source_refs.section: evt-073701 / Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila
source_refs.excerpt: Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila.

contradictions.label: limited_record_contradiction
contradictions.summary: Sources offer almost no narrative for Favila beyond succession and a few funerary notes; chroniclers treat him as minor.

source_refs.section: evt-087901 / Puesto que de Favila no hay mucho más que contar
source_refs.excerpt: Puesto que de Favila no hay mucho más que contar, podemos dirigir la mirada hacia lo que pasaba en el resto de España...

relationships.summary: Son of Pelayo; direct dynastic successor.

source_refs.excerpt: Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila.

relationships.summary: Family connection: Ermesinda identified as daughter of Pelayo; Favila is her brother (implied).

source_refs.section: evt-073701 / mention of Ermesinda as daughter of Pelayo
source_refs.excerpt: Lo único que nos consta es que una hija de Pelayo, Ermesinda...

internal_needs.label: need_stability
internal_needs.summary: Favila would need to consolidate his father's authority into stable rule, but sources suggest he did not make a lasting imprint.

source_refs.section: evt-087901 / Puesto que de Favila no hay mucho más que contar
source_refs.excerpt: Puesto que de Favila no hay mucho más que contar...

likely_arc.label: short_reign_transitional_arc
likely_arc.summary: Likely arc: transitional ruler whose brief reign bridges the founder Pelayo and later more durable rulers; little independent agency recorded.

source_refs.excerpt: Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila.

source_refs.section: evt-089401 / Favila dejaba viuda y, al menos, una hija
source_refs.excerpt: Favila dejaba viuda y, al menos, una hija.

event_records.change_summary: Favila takes the throne after Pelayo's death; chroniclers give limited further detail.

observed_from_source.label: succession_recorded
observed_from_source.summary: Sources explicitly state Favila succeeded Pelayo upon Pelayo's death.

source_refs.excerpt: Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila.
Cuando murió Pelayo, en 737, le sucedió su hijo Favila.

characters.biography_short: Son of Pedro of Cantabria; ruler who consolidated and expanded the Asturian polity after the founding period, credited with territorial expansion and political organization.

visual_profile.clothing: early regal/ducal garments, mix of noble and military garb
visual_profile.style_notes: Depict as consolidator: more ordered, with clear heraldry and mounted presence in campaign scenes.

emotional_state.trigger_summary: Success in territorial extension and institutional consolidation; opportunity created by divisions among Muslim rulers.

arc_state.external_goal: consolidate and expand the territorial basis of Asturias
arc_state.internal_need: transform a precarious wartime collectivity into a stable polity
arc_state.contradiction: expansionist success but harsh methods implied by chronicles
arc_state.change_vector: from frontier war-leader to institutional sovereign

goals.label: territorial_expansion
goals.summary: Alfonso I extended control across the Cantabrian fringe—sources credit him with enlarging Asturias up to Vizcaya and Galicia.

source_refs.section: chk-0286 / Ante todo, dibujemos el paisaje. Alfonso deja a su muerte, en 757, un reino de Asturias ...
source_refs.excerpt: Alfonso deja a su muerte, en 757, un reino de Asturias que abarca toda la cornisa cantábrica ...

source_refs.section: evt-090501 / Alfonso I debió de ver el cielo abierto
source_refs.excerpt: Alfonso I debió de ver el cielo abierto, en efecto, porque la querella musulmana le permitía extender su control sobre territorios nuevos.

behavior_patterns.label: campaigns_and_no_garrisons
behavior_patterns.summary: When Alfonso's forces advanced they often did not leave garrisons; movement was rapid and aimed at shock rather than occupation.

source_refs.section: chk-0224 / Cuando los hombres del rey Alfonso llegaban a una ciudad o a un pueblo, no se detenían a ocupar el terreno.
source_refs.excerpt: Cuando los hombres del rey Alfonso llegaban a una ciudad o a un pueblo, no se detenían a ocupar el terreno. No podían exponerse a debilitar su fuerza de choque...

relationships.summary: Alfonso married Ermesinda (daughter of Pelayo) — established dynastic ties.

source_refs.section: evt-110701 / Nació de los amores de Alfonso I con una mujer llamada Sisalda ... Alfonso, hijo del duque Pedro de Cantabr
source_refs.excerpt: Recordemos: Alfonso, hijo del duque Pedro de Cantabria, se casa con Ermesinda, la hija de Don Pelayo.

internal_needs.label: need_statecraft
internal_needs.summary: Alfonso likely needed to build administrative structures (counts/comtes) to hold territory he conquered; sources imply delegating authority to local counts.

source_refs.section: evt-091001 / JOSÉ JAVIER ESPARZA via ... delegó la organización territorial en comtes
source_refs.excerpt: Delegó la organización territorial en comtes condes que representaban a la autoridad en sus respectivas áreas...

private_state.label: resolute_leader_private
private_state.summary: A private orientation toward decisive, at times ruthless, action in campaigns (inferred from descriptions of campaign methods).

source_refs.section: chk-0218 / Las campañas de Alfonso ... llegan a una población, mata a todos...
source_refs.excerpt: Las campañas de Alfonso en tierras del Duero son implacables; llega a una población, mata a todos...

likely_arc.label: war_leader_to_state_builder
likely_arc.summary: Arc: military leader who converts battlefield gains into territorial-political consolidation.

source_refs.section: chk-0286 / Ante todo, dibujemos el paisaje ... Alfonso deja a su muerte, en 757, un reino ...
source_refs.excerpt: Alfonso deja a su muerte, en 757, un reino de Asturias que abarca toda la cornisa cantábrica ...

event_records.change_summary: Alfonso's reign expanded the realm materially and began administrative consolidation.

observed_from_source.label: expansion_recorded
observed_from_source.summary: Sources note Alfonso extended control and left a considerably enlarged kingdom at his death in 757.

source_refs.section: chk-0286 / Ante todo, dibujemos el paisaje ...
source_refs.excerpt: Alfonso deja a su muerte, en 757, un reino de Asturias que abarca toda la cornisa cantábrica ...
Alfonso I debió de ver el cielo abierto ...

characters.biography_short: Daughter of Pelayo; later married Alfonso I (sources note her as a dynastic connector). Mentioned in succession and genealogical contexts; limited independent action recorded.

visual_profile.clothing: noble woman's dress of early Asturian court (inferred)
visual_profile.style_notes: Portray as dynastic bridge—noble, reserved, visually tied to both Pelayo's lineage and Alfonso's court.

emotional_state.trigger_summary: Role as dynastic link and caretaker of lineage in an unstable frontier environment.

arc_state.external_goal: ensure dynastic continuity and familial security
arc_state.contradiction: named in sources but with minimal agency recorded
arc_state.change_vector: from daughter of a war-leader to consort in a consolidating court

goals.label: dynastic_link
goals.summary: Ermesinda is recorded as a daughter of Pelayo and as wife of Alfonso I, making her a dynastic connector.

source_refs.section: evt-073701 / mention of Ermesinda as daughter
source_refs.excerpt: Lo único que nos consta es que una hija de Pelayo, Ermesinda...

source_refs.section: evt-110701 / Recordemos: Alfonso ... se casa con Ermesinda, la hija de Don Pelayo
source_refs.excerpt: Recordemos: Alfonso ... se casa con Ermesinda, la hija de Don Pelayo.
Lo único que nos consta es que una hija de Pelayo, Ermesinda...

relationships.summary: Wife/consort of Alfonso I (observed reference in genealogical passages).

source_refs.excerpt: Alfonso ... se casa con Ermesinda, la hija de Don Pelayo.

private_state.label: caretaker_role_inferred
private_state.summary: Likely private orientation toward preserving family memory and protecting heirs (inferred from dynastic mentions).

source_refs.section: evt-089601 / En aquel momento pocos había con tantos títulos como Alfonso
source_refs.excerpt: En aquel momento pocos había con tantos títulos como Alfonso para ceñir la corona. Recordemos que el núcleo inicial del reino se había formado ...

event_records.change_summary: Marriage to Alfonso I ties founding dynasty to subsequent ruling house.

observed_from_source.label: marriage_recorded
observed_from_source.summary: Sources explicitly link Ermesinda as wife of Alfonso I.

source_refs.excerpt: Recordemos: Alfonso ... se casa con Ermesinda, la hija de Don Pelayo.
Recordemos: Alfonso ... se casa con Ermesinda, la hija de Don Pelayo.

characters.biography_short: Named in the sources as a Muslim commander who led forces against the Asturian rebels, involved in the pitched engagement(s) that surround Covadonga-era fighting.

voice_profile.tone: formal, measured, commanding but not ornate

visual_profile.style_notes: Depict as disciplined commander of mounted infantry; emphasize tactical disadvantage in mountain terrain.

emotional_state.trigger_summary: Conducting punitive or punitive-style expeditions against localized rebellions.

arc_state.external_goal: reassert Muslim military dominance in contested northern zones
arc_state.internal_need: restore order and assert authority for Córdoba's governors
arc_state.contradiction: operational success elsewhere but difficulties in mountain warfare
arc_state.change_vector: from expedition leader to part of the wider Muslim administration story

goals.label: demonstration_of_force
goals.summary: Sources record Muslim commanders (identified as Alkama/Alkama variants) initiating combat to demonstrate force in northern engagements.

source_refs.section: evt-073801 / Alkama mandó entonces comenzar el combate
source_refs.excerpt: Alkama mandó entonces comenzar el combate, y los solda dos tomaron las armas
actors: don_oppas, al_qama, pelayo

behavior_patterns.label: direct_engagement
behavior_patterns.summary: Initiates pitched battles in response to rebel concentrations; conventional military approach unsuited to mountain terrain.

source_refs.excerpt: Los cristianos, pocos y sin alimentos; los moros, muchos y bien armados. Pero el terreno jugaba a favor de los cristianos...

relationships.summary: Opposed Pelayo's rebel band in battlefield engagements.

source_refs.excerpt: actors: don_oppas, al_qama, pelayo

internal_needs.label: assert_authority
internal_needs.summary: As a commander, needed to assert provincial/military authority to prevent contagion of rebellion.

source_refs.section: evt-072201 / Las hostilidades se enquistan ... nuevo gobernador, Anbasa ... doblado los impuestos
source_refs.excerpt: Las hostilidades se enquistan ... Hay en Córdoba un nuevo gobernador, Anbasa, que dobló los impuestos a...

likely_arc.label: commander_frustrated_by_terrain
likely_arc.summary: Likely arc: competent field commander whose methods are occasionally frustrated by the special conditions of the Cantabrian mountains.

source_refs.excerpt: Pero el terreno jugaba a favor de los cristianos: mover a un... era arriesgarse al colapso.

event_records.change_summary: Engaged in pitched battle with rebels; terrain and tactics led to disordered retreat in sources.

observed_from_source.label: battle_engagement_recorded
observed_from_source.summary: Sources list al_qama/Alkama among commanders in the engagement described as 'se planteó la batalla'.

source_refs.excerpt: Así, en fin, se planteó la batalla. actors: don_oppas, al_qama, pelayo
actors: don_oppas, al_qama, pelayo

characters.biography_short: A figure identified on the Muslim/visigothic side present in narratives of the pitched engagements against Pelayo's rebels; sources place him among commanders at relevant encounters.

visual_profile.clothing: clerical/official garb when in court; commander dress in campaign depictions (inferred)
visual_profile.style_notes: When used visually, emphasize his ambiguous position between ecclesiastical and political roles.

emotional_state.trigger_summary: Participates as commander or notable figure in engagements recorded in chronicles.

arc_state.contradiction: presence in sources but limited characterization
arc_state.change_vector: participant in military events rather than central actor

goals.label: participant_in_engagement
goals.summary: Listed among actors in the pitched battle descriptions around Covadonga-era fights.

source_refs.excerpt: actors: don_oppas, al_qama, pelayo

relationships.summary: Opposed Pelayo's rebel group in battle narratives.

source_refs.excerpt: actors: don_oppas, al_qama, pelayo

event_records.change_summary: Actor on the anti-rebel side in the pitched engagement; precise fate not specified.

observed_from_source.label: battle_actor_list
observed_from_source.summary: Don Oppas appears among the named actors in descriptions of the battle.

source_refs.excerpt: actors: don_oppas, al_qama, pelayo
actors: don_oppas, al_qama, pelayo

characters.biography_short: A Muslim governor mentioned in sources as involved with northern affairs; traditions report he attempted to incorporate Pelayo's sister into his household (political marriage/concubinage narratives appear in chronicles).

visual_profile.clothing: governor's tunic, richer fabrics when in court (inferred)
visual_profile.style_notes: Depict as provincial governor with hints of Islamic courtly dress when relevant.

emotional_state.trigger_summary: Role as provincial authority with interest in consolidating power via personal alliances.

arc_state.external_goal: secure provincial control and establish alliances (including via marriage/concubinage)
arc_state.contradiction: sources relay courtly/personal anecdotes (e.g., intent to incorporate a sister into a harem) that may mix fact and legend
arc_state.change_vector: from provincial governor to a figure in cross-cultural narratives

goals.label: governor_action_record
goals.summary: Sources mention Munuza as a governor involved in northern affairs; specifically accused in chronicles of wanting to incorporate Pelayo's sister into his household.

source_refs.section: evt-085201 / Explica también que el gobernador moro, Munuza, quisiera incorporar a su harén a la hermana de Pelayo
source_refs.excerpt: Explica también que el gobernador moro, Munuza, quisiera incorporar a su harén a la hermana de Pelayo, Adosinda, como forma de emparentar con los dueños de la tierra.

contradictions.label: legendary_personal_motivation
contradictions.summary: The account of Munuza's desire to take a sister of Pelayo into his household may reflect a literary trope (marriage/concubinage as political tool) and should be treated cautiously.

source_refs.excerpt: Explica también que el gobernador moro, Munuza, quisiera incorporar a su harén a la hermana de Pelayo ...

behavior_patterns.label: use_of_marriage_to_secure_control
behavior_patterns.summary: Reportedly attempted to secure influence through personal incorporation (marital/concubinage ties) with local elites.

source_refs.excerpt: Explica también que el gobernador moro, Munuza, quisiera incorporar a su harén a la hermana de Pelayo, Adosinda, como forma de emparentar con los dueños de la tierra.

relationships.summary: Interacted with Pelayo's family in a manner recorded by chronicles (attempt at incorporation of Pelayo's sister).

source_refs.excerpt: Explica también que el gobernador moro, Munuza, quisiera incorporar a su harén a la hermana de Pelayo, Adosinda...

internal_needs.label: need_influence_via_personal_alliances
internal_needs.summary: As a provincial governor, Munuza would have motive to secure local ties via marriage or concubinage to stabilize control.

source_refs.excerpt: Explica también que el gobernador moro, Munuza, quisiera incorporar a su harén a la hermana de Pelayo...

event_records.change_summary: Reported attempt to create kinship ties with local elites by assimilating a member of Pelayo's family; mixes political goals and personal narrative.

observed_from_source.label: harem_attempt_recorded
observed_from_source.summary: Sources recount Munuza's intent to incorporate Pelayo's sister into his household as a way of linking with local owners of land.

source_refs.excerpt: Explica también que el gobernador moro, Munuza, quisiera incorporar a su harén a la hermana de Pelayo, Adosinda...
Explica también que el gobernador moro, Munuza, quisiera incorporar a su harén a la hermana de Pelayo...

characters.biography_short: King (or leader) associated in sources with factional struggles preceding the Muslim invasion; his factional politics are used in chronicles to explain later alignments and hostilities.

visual_profile.style_notes: When referenced, used as background political cause of later strife rather than as active contemporary actor to Pelayo's story.

emotional_state.trigger_summary: Factional disputes and alleged causes of collaboration with invading forces (as expressed in chronicles).

arc_state.external_goal: maintain/extend power within a fracturing Visigothic polity
arc_state.contradiction: sources use Witiza as a narrative cause for later conflicts but documentary evidence is partial

goals.label: factional_power
goals.summary: Witiza and the witizian faction figure in chronicles as catalysts for internal strife; his accession and factional policies are cited as reasons some nobles opposed his line.

source_refs.section: evt-070201 / A partir de ese momento, los hijos de Favila quedan enfrentados a Witiza
source_refs.excerpt: A partir de ese momento, los hijos de Favila quedan enfrentados a Witiza. Y cuando Witiza suba al trono, en 702, Pelayo tendrá que salir a escape.

source_refs.section: chk-0073 / Dice la leyenda que Don Julián guardaba rencor ...
source_refs.excerpt: Dice la leyenda que Don Julián guardaba rencor a Don Rodrigo ... Esto es tradición y no hay prueba de tal cosa.

relationships.summary: Witiza's accession and factional policy placed him in opposition to the descendants of Favila/Pelayo in source narratives.

source_refs.excerpt: A partir de ese momento, los hijos de Favila quedan enfrentados a Witiza.

event_records.change_summary: Witiza's rise exacerbated existing factional tensions and is used by chronicles to explain later displacements and exiles.

observed_from_source.label: witiza_faction_recorded
observed_from_source.summary: Sources connect Witiza's accession to internal strife among visigothic elites and to the dispossession/exile of certain families.

source_refs.excerpt: A partir de ese momento, los hijos de Favila quedan enfrentados a Witiza.
A partir de ese momento, los hijos de Favila quedan enfrentados a Witiza.
